message:
The other day, I found myself in a bit of a "I told you so" moment. A few weeks back, I had nudged my family to pack go-bags just in case of emergencies. You know, the usual - some water, snacks, important documents, and a few comfort items. My kids rolled their eyes, thinking it was just another one of my preparedness quirks. 

Fast forward to a surprise storm that knocked out our power for a couple of days. As we huddled together with flashlights, I pulled out our go-bags and we were all grateful for the snacks and games inside. My husband even jokingly called me a "preparedness guru" (I’ll take it!). 

In the end, it was a cozy family bonding experience, but it also reminded me how important it is to be ready for the unexpected. Sometimes, those little nudges towards preparedness can come in handy! 

What’s one time you were glad you were prepared for something unexpected?
